title: Ambientdensity
summary: Defines homogeneous ambient densities and their restriction to conformal densities on the null cone.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines homogeneous ambient densities and their restriction to conformal densities on the null cone.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/Ambientdensity.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: the ambient weight is defined to be Conformal Structure.Weight α : equals α.

An ambient density is an equivariant function on the ambient bundle. Its mathematical data are: to Fun: Conf.Ambient Bundle maps to α; equivariant: for every r : Pos Real U : Conf.Ambient Bundle, to Fun Conf.ambient Dilate r U equals χ r times to Fun U; instance: Coe Fun Density Conf : equals Conf χ fun maps to Conf.Ambient Bundle maps to α where coe d : equals d.to Fun @ simp theorem equivariant apply d : Density Conf : equals Conf χ r : Pos Real U : Conf.Ambient Bundle : d Conf.ambient Dilate r U equals χ r times d U : equals d.equivariant r U @ ext theorem ext d₁ d₂ : Density Conf : equals Conf χ h : for every U : Conf.Ambient Bundle, d₁.to Fun U equals d₂.to Fun U : d₁ equals d₂ : equals by cases d₁ with | mk f₁ h₁ maps to cases d₂ with | mk f₂ h₂ maps to have hf : f₁ equals f₂ : equals funext h cases hf have hh : h₁ equals h₂ : equals by apply Subsingleton.elim cases hh rfl.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{toFun}&\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle} \longrightarrow α\\\operatorname{equivariant}&\colon \forall (r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (U \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle}), \operatorname{toFun} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{ambientDilate} r U) = χ r \cdot \operatorname{toFun} U\\\operatorname{instance}&\colon \operatorname{CoeFun} (\operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (\operatorname{fun} _ => \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle} \longrightarrow α) \operatorname{where} \operatorname{coe} d = d.\operatorname{toFun} @[\operatorname{simp}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{equivariant\_apply} (d \colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (U \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle}) \colon d (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{ambientDilate} r U) = χ r \cdot d U = d.\operatorname{equivariant} r U @[\operatorname{ext}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{ext} {d₁ d₂ \colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ} (h \colon \forall U \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle}, d₁.\operatorname{toFun} U = d₂.\operatorname{toFun} U) \colon d₁ = d₂ = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{cases} d₁ \operatorname{with} | \operatorname{mk} f₁ h₁ => \operatorname{cases} d₂ \operatorname{with} | \operatorname{mk} f₂ h₂ => \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hf} \colon f₁ = f₂ = \operatorname{funext} h \operatorname{cases} \operatorname{hf} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hh} \colon h₁ = h₂ = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{apply} \operatorname{Subsingleton}.\operatorname{elim} \operatorname{cases} \operatorname{hh} \operatorname{rfl}\end{aligned}

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Restriction of an ambient density to the hypersurface ρ = 0. In explicit terms, for d of type density conf : equals conf χ, its defining expression is to Fun : equals the function sending u to d.to Fun Conf.iota u equivariant : equals by intro r u simpa Conformal Structure.iota, Conformal Structure.ambient Dilate, Conformal Structure.dilate Bundle using d.equivariant r Conf.iota u.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{restrictToBoundary}(d)=\operatorname{toFun} = u \longmapsto d.\operatorname{toFun} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{iota} u) \operatorname{equivariant} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} r u \operatorname{simpa} [\operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{iota}, \operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{ambientDilate}, \operatorname{ConformalStructure}.\operatorname{dilateBundle}] \operatorname{using} d.\operatorname{equivariant} r (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{iota} u)
correspondence.leanType: def restrictToBoundary (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : ConformalStructure.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Restriction to the boundary, evaluated on the section of a chosen scale. In explicit terms, for d of type density conf : equals conf χ, its defining expression is restrict To Boundary d : equals d.to Scale Dependent.
correspondence.leanType: def restrictToScaleDependent (d : Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : ConformalStructure.Density (Conf := Conf) χ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A concrete ambient density is an equivariant function on the concrete ambient bundle. Its mathematical data are: to Fun: Conf.Ambient Point Metric Bundle maps to α; equivariant: for every r : Pos Real U : Conf.Ambient Point Metric Bundle, to Fun Conf.ambient Point Dilate r U equals χ r times to Fun U; instance: Coe Fun Density Conf : equals Conf χ fun maps to Conf.Ambient Point Metric Bundle maps to α where coe d : equals d.to Fun @ simp theorem equivariant apply d : Density Conf : equals Conf χ r : Pos Real U : Conf.Ambient Point Metric Bundle : d Conf.ambient Point Dilate r U equals χ r times d U : equals d.equivariant r U @ ext theorem ext d₁ d₂ : Density Conf : equals Conf χ h : for every U : Conf.Ambient Point Metric Bundle, d₁.to Fun U equals d₂.to Fun U : d₁ equals d₂ : equals by cases d₁ with | mk f₁ h₁ maps to cases d₂ with | mk f₂ h₂ maps to have hf : f₁ equals f₂ : equals funext h cases hf have hh : h₁ equals h₂ : equals by apply Subsingleton.elim cases hh rfl.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}\operatorname{toFun}&\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientPointMetricBundle} \longrightarrow α\\\operatorname{equivariant}&\colon \forall (r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (U \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientPointMetricBundle}), \operatorname{toFun} (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{ambientPointDilate} r U) = χ r \cdot \operatorname{toFun} U\\\operatorname{instance}&\colon \operatorname{CoeFun} (\operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (\operatorname{fun} _ => \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientPointMetricBundle} \longrightarrow α) \operatorname{where} \operatorname{coe} d = d.\operatorname{toFun} @[\operatorname{simp}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{equivariant\_apply} (d \colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ) (r \colon \operatorname{PosReal}) (U \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientPointMetricBundle}) \colon d (\operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{ambientPointDilate} r U) = χ r \cdot d U = d.\operatorname{equivariant} r U @[\operatorname{ext}] \operatorname{theorem} \operatorname{ext} {d₁ d₂ \colon \operatorname{Density} (\operatorname{Conf} = \operatorname{Conf}) χ} (h \colon \forall U \colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientPointMetricBundle}, d₁.\operatorname{toFun} U = d₂.\operatorname{toFun} U) \colon d₁ = d₂ = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{cases} d₁ \operatorname{with} | \operatorname{mk} f₁ h₁ => \operatorname{cases} d₂ \operatorname{with} | \operatorname{mk} f₂ h₂ => \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hf} \colon f₁ = f₂ = \operatorname{funext} h \operatorname{cases} \operatorname{hf} \operatorname{have} \operatorname{hh} \colon h₁ = h₂ = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{apply} \operatorname{Subsingleton}.\operatorname{elim} \operatorname{cases} \operatorname{hh} \operatorname{rfl}\end{aligned}

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
